description: REQ-EDIT-001 / PROP-EDIT-025 mandates that clicking a Block dispatches FocusBlock exactly ONCE per focus transition. The implementation has TWO independent focus side-effect paths that can both fire on a single click: (a) BlockElement.svelte:95-101 `handleClick` calls `blockEl.focus()` which triggers `onfocusin → adapter.dispatchFocusBlock` (this is the one production browsers exercise); (b) EditorPanel.svelte:186-193 `$effect` reacts to `viewState.focusedBlockId` and calls `el.focus()` on the matching block; the `el.focus()` triggers focusin which dispatches FocusBlock from BlockElement again. When a domain snapshot lands with the same focusedBlockId the user just clicked, the $effect re-focuses the same element. Although a guard `document.activeElement !== el` prevents the no-op case, race conditions (rapid clicks or switching back to a previously focused block) can produce duplicate FocusBlock dispatches. There is no debounce on FocusBlock dispatch and no test that asserts exactly-once dispatch under simultaneous click+snapshot scenarios.
suggestedRemediation: Add an integration test: click block-2 (when block-1 was focused), wait one tick, emit a snapshot with focusedBlockId='block-2', flushSync, assert dispatchFocusBlock called exactly once. Alternatively, when the domain echoes the user's click via snapshot, the $effect should be inert because `document.activeElement` already equals the target. Verify this guard works under jsdom via test, since jsdom's focus management differs from real browsers.
